About: It is about American Civil War naval combat.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Yaquinto Publications.
ConsistsOf: It consists of 300 die cut unit counters.
It consists of a 4-piece isomorphic mapboard, 42" x 27.5".
It consists of a log pad.
It consists of charts and tables.
It consists of two six-sided dice.
It consists of a plastic tray for counters.
It consists of 45 double-sided ship specification cards.
It consists of a rule book.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by John W. Fuseler.
HasA: It has unique armament and armor for each ship.
It has scenarios presented as a two-ship combat.
It has one scenario that is a solitaire game.
It has several scenarios that present multiplayer fleet engagements.
It has rules that were initially problematic.
It has an expansion kit published the following year.
It has ship silhouettes on counters that were changed from overhead to sideview in a republication.
It has a subtitle: "A Tactical Level Game of Naval Combat in the American Civil War 1861–1865".
IsA: It is a game that simulates naval warfare between ironclads during the American Civil War.
It is a simulation of naval warfare during the American Civil War.
It is a rewrite and extension of The Ironclads.
KnownFor: It is known for being an exciting, accurate simulation of naval warfare during the American Civil War.
It is known for its problematic rules in the first edition.
It is known for being awarded the Charles S. Roberts Award for "Best Initial Release of 1979".
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the American Civil War 1861–1865.
PartOf: It is part of Yaquinto Games' inaugural games.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Yaquinto Publications.
PublishedAs: It was published as "A Tactical Level Game of Naval Combat in the American Civil War 1861–1865".
PublishedBy: It was published by Yaquinto Publications.
It was published by 3W (World Wide Wargames).
UsedAs: It is used as a standalone game.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating American Civil War naval combat.
It is used for simulating naval warfare between ironclads during the American Civil War.
It is used for combat resolution.
It is used for incorporating the original game.
UsedIn: It is used in scenarios set in South America and Europe.
It is used in optional combat rules.
UsedTo: It is used to extend the timeline of battles to 1879.
It is used to add ships from European nations.
WonBy: It was won by the Charles S. Roberts Award for "Best Initial Release of 1979".
WrittenBy: It was written by John W. Fuseler.
It was written by Roger Nord.
It was written by Jack Greene.
